reasons: Baxia directly reduces the healing value that helps Khaleed survive trades.
He can contest Khaleed's short-range brawling without being easily forced out.

proof.summary: Khaleed wants to heal and reset through Quicksand Guard, while Baxia reduces HP regeneration effects when applying Baxia Mark through skills.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps anti-regen uptime during Khaleed's healing window.
The team punishes Khaleed while Quicksand Guard is interrupted or reduced.
failureCases: Khaleed disengages before the anti-regen matters.
Baxia cannot match Khaleed's rotation timing.
Khaleed wins through early burst before Baxia stabilizes.

reasons: Valir can push Khaleed away during Desert Tornado or before Khaleed reaches the backline.
Valir's cleanse and movement window helps him survive Khaleed's engage attempt.

proof.summary: Khaleed wants to close distance and force short-range trades, while Valir can knock him back, slow him, and kite the engage.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves Searing Torrent for Khaleed's entry.
Teammates kite backward instead of fighting inside Khaleed's range.
failureCases: Valir wastes knockback before Khaleed commits.

reasons: Dyrroth can challenge Khaleed's lane dominance with defense reduction and burst.
Khaleed prefers sustained trades, while Dyrroth can make short trades dangerous.

proof.summary: Khaleed wants to brawl and heal through lane trades, while Dyrroth can reduce physical defense and burst him before he resets.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth trades around enhanced skill timing.
Khaleed's Quicksand Guard is unavailable or interrupted.
failureCases: Khaleed kites out and heals before Dyrroth finishes.
Khaleed wins early tempo and denies Dyrroth farm.

reasons: Karrie can punish Khaleed if he builds durable and tries to stay in extended fights.
Her value increases when teammates keep Khaleed away from direct access.

proof.summary: Khaleed wants to survive as a bruiser, while Karrie converts repeated hits into max-HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Khaleed is forced to chase through open space.
failureCases: Khaleed reaches Karrie first with Raging Sandstorm.
Khaleed's team controls Karrie before she stacks marks.

reasons: Lunox can pressure Khaleed through magic penetration and burst windows.
Khaleed is vulnerable if he cannot safely channel sustain or close distance.

proof.summary: Khaleed wants to sustain through damage, while Lunox can threaten him with penetration-based magic burst from safer spacing.
worksBestWhen: Lunox keeps distance and avoids Khaleed's ultimate path.
failureCases: Khaleed reaches Lunox before she cycles skills.
Lunox is forced defensive too early.
Khaleed's team follows up on the engage.
